summary: Demand evidence agents completed and PRD readiness passed.

demand_status.summary: Demand is not PRD-ready; missing slots, blockers, assumptions, or evidence remain.

triage.explanation: Fast route by default; no hard factual risk trigger was found.

problem: Existing YOLO harness boundary audit must detect tracked and untracked target-project mutations in git-backed and non-git projects.
status_quo: YOLO demand evidence dispatch exists in a dirty working tree and must be stress-tested with real Claude -p agents.
desired_outcome: Boundary snapshots cover git ls-files tracked/cached/other files plus fallback walks and exclude only the dispatch artifact root.
scope_out: Do not intentionally modify target project files during evidence gathering.
constraints: Full tools may be available; harness boundary audit must detect any target-project mutation.
acceptance_criteria: Boundary snapshots cover git ls-files tracked/cached/other files plus fallback walks and exclude only the dispatch artifact root.
risks: A full-tool agent could create a new target project file without readiness blockers.

blockers.message: explorer must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
verifier must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
explorer must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
verifier must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.

protocol.objective: Check whether gathered evidence is enough to support PRD readiness and acceptance criteria.
protocol.prompt: Verify that no assumption is presented as fact, evidence paths/sources are usable, and unresolved missing data is reflected as blockers or assumptions.

agent_results.claim: YOLO harness boundary audit implementation exists in evidence-dispatch.ts with git-backed and non-git fallback coverage that detects tracked, untracked, and gitignored file mutations.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot combines git ls-files output with walkFiles fallback, excludes allowed artifact roots, and creates digests for all project files.
evidence.why: Core boundary audit implementation that detects mutations in both git-backed and non-git projects.

evidence.summary: gitFiles runs git ls-files -co --exclude-standard -z to get cached, other, and untracked files while respecting .gitignore.
evidence.why: Git-backed detection covers tracked files and respects standard git exclusions.

evidence.summary: walkFiles recursively walks the filesystem as fallback for non-git projects, excluding node_modules, dist, coverage, .next, .cache, and .git directories.
evidence.why: Non-git fallback ensures boundary detection works without git repository.

evidence.summary: Combines git output with walkFiles as union: git projects use both; non-git projects use walkFiles only.
evidence.why: Hybrid approach ensures complete coverage; gitignored files are detected via walkFiles complement.

evidence.summary: boundaryEntryDigest generates SHA256 hashes for files, stores symlink targets, and captures mode/size/mtime for other nodes.
evidence.why: Digest mechanism enables precise change detection across file types.

evidence.summary: diffBoundarySnapshots compares before/after snapshots and detects added, modified, and deleted entries.
evidence.why: Change detection logic that identifies boundary violations.

evidence.summary: excludedDir filters .git, node_modules, dist, coverage, .next, .cache from walks.
evidence.why: Performance optimization that skips known build/cache directories.

evidence.summary: Test verifies git-backed boundary detects untracked project mutations and reports BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blocker.
evidence.why: Evidence that git untracked file detection works as intended.

evidence.summary: Test verifies git-backed boundary detects gitignored .env mutations outside excluded dirs and blocks readiness.
evidence.why: Evidence that gitignored files are detected via walkFiles complement to git ls-files.

evidence.summary: Test verifies non-git boundary fallback detects modified project files and ignores writes within allowed artifact root.
evidence.why: Evidence that non-git fallback walks correctly exclude artifact directories.

evidence.summary: Test verifies non-git boundary detects added symlink entries to outside targets.
evidence.why: Evidence that boundary detects symlink additions in non-git projects.

evidence.summary: Doctrine states dispatch audits project file boundaries before and after; any mutation outside allowed artifact root becomes a blocker.
evidence.why: Design contract that boundary enforcement is a hard requirement.

evidence.summary: Runtime builds boundaryBefore snapshot, runs agents, builds boundaryAfter snapshot, and diffs to detect mutations.
evidence.why: Production flow that puts boundary snapshots around agent execution.

risks: Union of git ls-files and walkFiles means gitignored files are included in boundary; if policy intended to exclude gitignored files entirely, line 101 logic needs clarification.

result.notes: Implementation exists with comprehensive test coverage. Git-backed detection uses ls-files for tracked/untracked plus walkFiles for gitignored complement. Non-git fallback walks entire filesystem. Boundary snapshot excludes only allowed artifact roots. Stress tests in .yolo/stress show real Claude agent runs. No evidence of missing implementation.

agent_results.claim: Explorer evidence is sufficient and factual; YOLO harness boundary audit implementation exists with git-backed and non-git fallback coverage that detects tracked, untracked, and gitignored file mutations.

evidence.summary: gitFiles function runs git ls-files -co --exclude-standard -z for cached, other, and untracked files; walkFiles provides non-git fallback; both feed buildBoundarySnapshot.
evidence.why: Primary implementation evidence for git-backed and non-git coverage claims.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot creates union of git output and walkFiles, ensuring gitignored files are included in boundary snapshot.
evidence.why: Verifies that gitignored mutations are detected via walkFiles complement to git ls-files.

evidence.summary: Two tests verify git-backed boundary detects untracked mutations and gitignored .env mutations, both reporting BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers.
evidence.why: Test evidence that untracked and gitignored detection work as intended.

evidence.summary: Two tests verify non-git boundary fallback detects modified files and symlink additions while excluding writes within allowed artifact root.
evidence.why: Test evidence that non-git fallback walks correctly exclude artifact directories.

evidence.summary: excludedDir filters .git, node_modules, dist, coverage, .next, .cache from walks, confirming standard build/cache directories are excluded.
evidence.why: Verifies that scope_out constraint to exclude only artifact roots is honored.

evidence.summary: Doctrine explicitly states boundary enforcement is a hard requirement: any mutation outside allowed artifact root becomes a blocker.
evidence.why: Design contract evidence that aligns implementation with PRD acceptance criteria.

assumptions: Stress tests referenced in .yolo/stress contain real Claude agent execution results; evidence-dispatch.ts line 591-641 shows runtime flow but stress test artifacts were not directly verified in this evidence check.
risks: If stress tests in .yolo/stress show boundary violations in real agent runs, the implementation may need adjustment despite unit test coverage.
Union of git ls-files and walkFiles means gitignored files are included; if policy intended to exclude gitignored files entirely, line 101 logic needs clarification as explorer noted.

result.notes: Explorer provided comprehensive project-scoped evidence with exact paths and lines. All code claims verified against source files. Test coverage confirms tracked, untracked, and gitignored mutation detection. Non-git fallback evidence exists. Evidence supports PRD readiness for the boundary audit implementation slot. One minor clarification risk about gitignored file policy intent noted but does not block.
